concept: Guts / The Inner Workings / The Belly's Coils
description: The guts: the belly's inner workings — intestines and the deep interior generally. With 'kumoe' (stomach) and 'mokura' (belly) it finishes the abdomen. Also, as everywhere, the seat of deep knowing: gut feeling translates directly.
sound_symbolism: 'nu' the deep, 'lo' the winding, 'mi' the innermost — the interior traced inward.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'nulomi nuora kuremi.' (The guts grind the food.) Figurative: 'mia mua nulomi sano.' (I know it in my guts.)

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: The deep interior digests experience as well as food; the tradition locates courage and knowing below the mind for good reason.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Gut-knowledge was respected knowledge: the deep interior as the seat of the knowing that precedes words.
